title: quercus suber subsp. occidentalis
intro: Quercus suber subsp. occidentalis, also known as the Western Cork Oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus suber tree, native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus suber subsp. occidentalis include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the bark or leaves, which are used to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues and respiratory problems.
